query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "joyai-vl-interaction: real-time vision-language interaction intelligen" computer science
rationale: The candidate paper focuses on safety mechanisms (jailbreak defense) for Multimodal Large Language Models, which is a distinct research construct from the user's question regarding "JoyAI-VL-Interaction," a system for real-time vision-language interaction intelligence. While both involve MLLMs, the paper does not measure the same mechanism, variables, or empirical population related to real-time interaction intelligence, nor does it serve as a foundational reference for that specific domain.
